six_oracle_facts.label: round-4-blocked-prerequisite-finding
six_oracle_facts.description: Round v4 has an unresolved significant correctness finding (CF-9D56C033A0AFFA4A7607) concerning the plan's optional CI/check-mode fetch path conflicting with the observe-only requirement to detect Git/provider mutation. This is a blocked-prerequisite-class finding because it concerns the gate admission semantics: the prerequisite verifier must not rely on network fetches during observe-only validation.
six_oracle_facts.evidence: In v4 gate_signals, unresolved_flags includes CF-9D56C033A0AFFA4A7607 with concern: 'The plan's optional CI/check-mode fetch path conflicts with the observe-only requirement to detect Git/provider mutation.'

six_oracle_facts.label: four-distinct-round-5-lens-occurrences
six_oracle_facts.description: Round v5 evaluator verdict contains 21 flag_verifications across four distinct lenses: correctness, scope, verification, and prerequisite_ordering. These are four distinct occurrence families representing separate evaluator review dimensions.

six_oracle_facts.label: nested-signals-recurring-critiques-empty
six_oracle_facts.description: In all five rounds (v1-v5), the gate_signals.signals.recurring_critiques field is an empty array, indicating that the evaluator found no recurring critique patterns across rounds that required special handling.
six_oracle_facts.interpretation: No recurring critique signals detected across rounds — each round's findings are treated as distinct occurrences.

six_oracle_facts.label: five-occurrences-reconciled-to-one-finding
six_oracle_facts.description: Five distinct critique occurrences (flag_verification entries) across rounds v1-v5 are reconciled to the same canonical finding CF-CD1C58FBC288E3BBA77C via FindingReconciliationEvent. The occurrences share the same semantic concern (scope/work sizing) and are reconciled as a single cross-round finding.
six_oracle_facts.reconciliation_note: This finding appears in v4 critique_custody with finding_count=6. The 5-to-1 reconciliation claim encodes that five distinct evaluator observations across rounds converge to this one semantic finding, represented as a FindingReconciliationEvent with relationship=DUPLICATE/REFINEMENT. The finding concerns incomplete scope decomposition (god-tasks) across multiple plan revision cycles.

six_oracle_facts.label: accepted-unavailable-replay-limitation-and-reopen-rule
six_oracle_facts.description: The M6 corpus captures prompts and completions with deterministic path redaction. Raw prompt/completion text for free-form critique material is available via the preserved repository at the exact source revision. If the preserved repository is unavailable, the redacted corpus provides content-addressed hashes sufficient for integrity verification but not for full semantic replay. The reopen condition is: restore preserved repository at revision ea2be1fe and re-run generate_cl1_m6_corpus.py.
six_oracle_facts.reopen_condition: Restore preserved repository at revision ea2be1fe36c42c4f19afedd2c096b5dcec7c56df and re-run generate_cl1_m6_corpus.py.

six_oracle_facts.label: failed-dropped-malformed-no-additional-representations
six_oracle_facts.description: Across v1-v5, all critique attempts produced valid findings (no FAILED, DROPPED, or MALFORMED parse statuses). Each round has distinct critique findings with no NO_ADDITIONAL_FINDINGS outcomes. The evaluator's 'skipped' selections (4-5 per round) represent dimensions not selected for review, not failed attempts.
six_oracle_facts.note: Skipped selections represent deferred review dimensions, not failures. Each round's evaluator selected 4-5 lenses for active review and skipped the remainder.

oracle_assertions.deterministic_generation: Running generate_cl1_m6_corpus.py twice produces byte-identical output (confirmed with diff).
oracle_assertions.revision_pinning: Corpus is pinned to exact commit ea2be1fe36c42c4f19afedd2c096b5dcec7c56df, verified via git cat-file -t.
oracle_assertions.rounds_coverage: All five rounds (v1-v5) are fully inventoried with all required artifacts present.
oracle_assertions.path_redaction: Deterministic regex-based redaction of workspace paths; identical input produces identical redacted output.
oracle_assertions.dual_hashing: Both raw and redacted SHA-256 hashes are computed and stored.
oracle_assertions.tamper_detection: --check mode compares regenerated output against existing fixture; any hash mismatch indicates tampering.
